description: Replace all model imagery with authentic Nigerian/African models in true Nigerian attire, remove 'West African Heritage Set' and replace with a Nigerian-origin design, update the Hero featured image to a Nigerian model in a Nigerian gown, and enrich the designs catalog with authentic Nigerian fashion descriptions.
steps: In src/data/designs.ts, replace the 'West African Heritage Set' (HF-003, pexels ID 31486360) with a new design called 'Igbo Bridal Blouse & Wrapper' or 'Aso-Oke Iro & Buba Set' - a truly Nigerian-origin design. Use a Pexels photo of a Black African/Nigerian woman in traditional Nigerian attire. Suggested Pexels IDs to research: search for 'african woman ankara', 'nigerian bride', 'african woman traditional dress'. Use IDs like 8386668, 7869226, 8146962, 6831896 or similar that show Black African women in authentic Nigerian attire.

Update the Hero section image (currently pexels ID 30030552) to a Pexels photo of a Nigerian/African woman in an elegant Nigerian gown (aso-ebi, or flowing Nigerian dinner gown). Change the featured text from 'Royal Aso-Ebi Set' to something like 'Emerald Aso-Oke Ensemble' or 'Luxury Ankara Gown'. Use pexels IDs that show Black African women: try 7869226, 6831896, 8386668, 5934222 (already used for About), or other verified Black African model photos.

Review ALL other pexels photo IDs used in designs.ts (IDs: 30030552, 3869639, 15020799, 19209599, 5248020, 17980365, 5934222, 34660898) and ensure they feature Black African/Nigerian models. Replace any that appear to show non-African models with alternatives. Specifically verify: 3869639 (Ankara Power Co-ord), 15020799 (Sunrise Day Dress), 19209599 (Boardroom Power Suit), 5248020 (Sunday Service Wrap), 17980365 (Onyx Gala Gown). Replace with Pexels photos of Black African models if needed.

Update the About section image (pexels ID 5934222 in src/components/About.tsx) - ensure it shows a Black African woman (designer at work or in her atelier). If the current one works, keep it; if not, replace with an appropriate alternative.

Update design descriptions in src/data/designs.ts to be more explicitly Nigerian in their language and cultural references. For example, reference specific Nigerian fabrics (george, aso-oke, adire), Nigerian ceremonies (traditional marriage, naming ceremony, chieftaincy), Nigerian cities (Lagos, Abuja, Enugu, Calabar).

Add 2-3 more designs to the catalog to make it richer - suggestions: 'Adire Shift Dress' (indigo-dyed Yoruba textile), 'George Wrapper & Blouse' (Igbo traditional), 'Senator Kaftan' (men's modern Nigerian). Use Pexels photos of Black African models in each case.
acceptance_criteria: 'West African Heritage Set' no longer exists in the catalog
A new Nigerian-origin design replaces it with an authentic name and description
Hero image shows a Black African model in Nigerian attire
All design images use Pexels IDs that feature Black African/Nigerian models
At least 11 designs total in the catalog (was 9, adding 2-3 new ones)
npx tsc -b passes with zero errors
findings: All 9 original Pexels photo IDs replaced with verified Black African/Nigerian model photos. Added 3 new designs (HF-010 through HF-012) bringing total to 12. Also updated OG image and schema.org image in index.html to match new hero photo. Build and TypeScript compilation both pass cleanly.
